key_insight: A physics-informed multihead neural network can learn a low-dimensional latent manifold of PDE solution families, revealing that only a few principal components capture most of the meaningful variation beyond the initial condition.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reproducible way to compress, compare, and interpret families of PDE solutions, which can help surrogate modeling, reduced-order simulation, and solution-space analysis.
method: Shared network body learns a latent embedding of solution families.
Linear heads reconstruct solutions for different initial conditions.
Head-orthogonalization penalty removes latent degeneracies and stabilizes the PCA spectrum; Fourier-shell analysis quantifies frequency contributions.
evidence_signal: On the one-dimensional viscous Burgers equation, a latent dimension of 20 yielded strong compression: about 2–4 principal components explained ~95% of latent variance and 4–7 explained ~99%, with similar behavior for heat and wave equations.
watch_item: Because the initial condition is built into the output by construction, the learned principal components describe residual variability rather than the full solution, so interpretation depends on the chosen parameterization and training stability.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Embeddings of PDE Solution Families
